description: This flow chart classifies Fe-Mn-Al-C steels into two main categories based on chemical composition, summarizes their mechanical property balance, and lists corresponding application areas. The first category is multiphase Fe-Mn-Al-C steels with 5-12 wt.% Mn and ≤ 0.8 wt.% C, which exhibits a strength range of 800–1200 MPa and elongation of 30–60%, and is applied in automotive structural components and reinforcements. The second category is austenitic Fe-Mn-Al-C steels with >12 wt.% Mn and 0.8-1.2 wt.% C, which shows a wider strength range of 600–1500 MPa and elongation lower than 60%, and is suitable for automotive structural components as well as military fields. The figure demonstrates that both types of Fe-Mn-Al-C steels possess favorable strength-ductility balance, supporting their great application potential in lightweight structural scenarios.
